definition: Use math to evaluate debt as a risk/coverage problem, not emotion.
rationale: Legitimate debt concepts mixed with delegation/key-man risk. Split enables distinct operational scaling domain.

splits.definition: Evaluate debt as volatility and coverage ratio problem rather than emotional fear.

members: Key Man Risk and Business Scaling
splits.definition: Remove founder dependency by decomposing role into composable positions and delegating.

definition: Define concepts via observable, measurable behavior rather than vague interpretation.
rationale: Cohesive cluster; all members reinforce the same principle of replacing vague language with falsifiable spec.

definition: Use premium pricing to fund premium talent acquisition and recruiting infrastructure.
rationale: Recruiting claims bundled with business-model economics. Split separates talent-specific challenges from broader model strategy.

splits.definition: A-talent is the binding constraint in scaling service businesses; hiring velocity and recruiting infrastructure determine growth ceiling.
Service businesses require higher margins than SaaS to absorb talent volatility and fund recruiting.

definition: Sell the next problem the customer discovers after their first purchase.
rationale: Tightly coherent; all members describe the same upsell pattern: solve next problem, add more, add more help.

definition: Founder's consistency, vision, and character determine which talent and investor caliber get attracted.
rationale: Cluster conflates sales reframing with founder psychology, brand perception, and co-founder strategy. Split into distinct domains.

members: Character and Vision in Team Building
splits.definition: Founder's reliability and vision determine caliber of talent and equity partners attracted.
Organic growth requires better founders; acquisition requires deal flow. Perception risk shapes founder behavior.

definition: Scale by going deeper on core strength, not by adding distracting service lines.
rationale: Exceptionally cohesive; every member reinforces specialization and focus as competitive advantage.

definition: Four paths to deploy accumulated capital: fund raising, venture investing, franchising, or reinvestment.
rationale: Broader than fund management alone. Canonical name captures the four distinct capital paths (franchising, fund management, investing, reinvestment) all members describe.

definition: The entrepreneur learns truth from market feedback faster than any mentor could teach.
rationale: Mixed themes (personal development, partnership, loneliness, business leverage). Split extracts core insight and separates partnership and loneliness as distinct concepts.

splits.definition: Entrepreneurship is fastest personal development vehicle; market feedback is truth friends won't give.
Partnerships only make sense if the partner fills a gap you cannot fill alone.
The season with neither peer support nor market validation is the truest test of founder conviction.
A business that stops entirely without the founder has leverage in negotiations but is not scalable.

definition: Founder outperforms systems by 30%; plan for this margin loss when delegating.
rationale: Remove Model Pivot (case study/example, not a claim about founder magic). Remaining members cohere around founder advantage decay.

definition: Four levers solve supply constraints: price, delivery ratio, productization, hiring. Choose by team bandwidth.
rationale: Exemplary cohesion. SPCL, Value Equation, Delivery Ratio, Lead Source Concentration all frame solutions via 4-lever models.

definition: Compress delivery into shorter sessions to split peak capacity; frame compression as convenience gain.
rationale: Tightly cohesive; all members address structuring delivery and creating urgency through time scarcity.

definition: Call canceling customers to uncover true obstacle and offer creative solution; saves ~25% of churn.
rationale: Exit interviews and objection handling are retention tactics. Market-shift and insurance-disruption are systemic-risk assessment, distinct domain.

splits.definition: Call canceling customers to uncover true obstacle, offer creative solution, save churn via proactive engagement.
Before pivoting model, test whether problem is systemic or segment-specific.

definition: Calculate income per activity per hour; ruthlessly delegate everything below your hourly threshold.
rationale: Tight focus on time-leverage math; all members address the same economic principle.

definition: Networking compounds; increase 3-5x before declaring it saturated.
rationale: Cohesive; all members reinforce that scaling networking via volume multiplies output and compounds.

definition: Wealthy customers evaluate price-to-value ratio; poor customers see only price tag.
rationale: Exemplary cohesion; every member addresses the same psychological principle across pricing, mindset, and buying behavior.
